summary: This week's AI news highlights significant developments in AI infrastructure, policy, and applications. The EU has ordered Google to open Android and Search to rivals, while NVIDIA has introduced new Jetson Thor computers for mainstream robotics and edge AI. OpenAI has launched a light-up keyboard for Codex, and Microsoft has patched a record number of security vulnerabilities using AI.
insight: The increasing use of AI in various industries has led to a growing need for specialized hardware and infrastructure, driving innovation and investment in this area.
highlights: Google must give rival AI assistants and search engines greater access to key parts of Android and Google Search
NVIDIA has introduced new Jetson Thor computers for mainstream robotics and edge AI
OpenAI has launched a light-up keyboard for Codex, its agentic coding assistant
